references.title: What Time is It? Student Modeling Needs to Know

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Prediction of completing the next task successfully
extracted.Target: Finish Assignment within 1 hour: Successful (0) vs Unsuccessful (1)
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Time is often not considered in models for KT in either self-paced or tutor-based education -time to be understood as time elapsed between sequential events.
extracted.Novelty of Model: T-LSTM introduce the ability to model time intervals and incorporate into the prediction
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Students who had requested hints were eliminated from the sample.

Code traces are timestamped snapshots of student code.

Features are extracted using a form of AutoML called Expert Feature Detector.
extracted.Models:
ZeroR,
Logistic Regression, 
Recent Temporal Pattern,
LTSM,
T-LSTM
extracted.Comments:
BKT was not considered for this open-ended task since there are no specific knowledge components or predefined steps that need to be followed.

Task on iSNAP is defined by the learners being able to solve a homework: a snapshop of their screen is taken every 2 minutes and this sequence is used for prediction.
extracted.Limitations:
Only a single modeling task per learning environment.

Small sample size.

No alternative methods to assess time-awareness.

extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Time is often not considered in models for KT in either self-paced or tutor-based education -time to be understood as time elapsed between sequential events.
extracted.Novelty of Model: T-LSTM introduce the ability to model time intervals and incorporate into the prediction
extracted.Models:
ZeroR,
Logistic Regression, 
BKT, LSTM, T-LSTM
extracted.Comments: Task on Pyrinees was to predict learning gains based on the sequences of answers given in a test.
extracted.Limitations:
Only a single modeling task per learning environment.

Small sample size.

No alternative methods to assess time-awareness.

annotations.text: Some of the most well known methods for KT are BKT and LSTMs. The problem with these approaches is that sub-divide time into discrete time-steps and not continuous. However, time between events  varies significantly and, fot that reason, authors decide to use Time-Aware LSTM for KT.

Authors explore T-LSTM in 2 domains. First, self-paced learning, wherein authors compare T-LSTM vs other standard classifiers to predict success. On a second-approach, they follow an instructor-paced setting and try to predict learning gains.
Results show LSTM beats all others in the self-paced setting and perform on par with LSTMs on instructor-paced learning.
